_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. SAF Attribution and Tracking Infrastructure**
The workspace must build or procure a capability to receive, record, and pass on SAF environmental attribute certificates at the shipment level. This requires: (a) an account on the IATA SAF Registry (available now; free registration); (b) a workflow to collect SAF batch certification data from airline partners on key lanes; (c) a mechanism to allocate SAF attributes to individual airway bills, by cargo weight or volume proportion. The SAF Registry was upgraded in September 2025 and early 2026 specifically for freight forwarder and intermediary workflows, including automatic CORSIA Eligible Fuel form generation. Scope: all air lanes, priority Americas/Europe/Asia. Deadline: operational before CORSIA First Phase compliance deadline (late 2027). Gap from baseline: likely significant — most forwarders lack systematic SAF attribute tracking today.

**2. CORSIA Cost and Surcharge Monitoring**
The workspace must implement a monitoring process for CORSIA EEU surcharges appearing in carrier tariffs and airfreight invoices. IATA projects sector-wide EEU cancellation costs of $4–5 billion for the First Phase (2024–2026), growing in the Second Phase. The workspace should: (a) establish a data field in its TMS/freight management system for CORSIA surcharge line items; (b) negotiate with airlines to receive itemised SAF/CORSIA cost breakdowns; (c) build client-facing disclosure templates for CORSIA-related cost components. Scope: all air cargo bookings. Deadline: immediate (surcharges are already appearing in 2025/2026 tariffs). Gap: moderate to significant depending on current TMS capability.

**3. Carrier Compliance Screening**
The workspace should onboard a periodic screening process to verify that airline partners on key trade lanes are participating in CORSIA (130 states as of 1 January 2026) and are obtaining SAF from ICAO Council-approved SCS-certified producers. This is particularly relevant for lanes through jurisdictions with active SAF mandates (EU, UK, Singapore from 2026, India from 2027). Supplier onboarding questionnaires for airline partners should include: CORSIA participation confirmation, SCS certification names for SAF batches used, and SAF volume share by route. Scope: all air carrier contracts. Deadline: incorporate into next contract renewal cycle or within 90 days for spot carriers.

**4. Contract Clause Updates**
All airfreight carrier agreements covering international routes should be reviewed and updated to include: (a) SAF cost transparency and itemisation obligations; (b) CORSIA MRV data-sharing clauses (CEF batch reference, lifecycle emissions value); (c) book-and-claim attribution rights where the workspace procures SAF on behalf of cargo clients; (d) indemnity provisions in the event of mis-certification of SAF batches by carriers or fuel suppliers. Legal confirmation is required on whether any clause creates 'economic operator' status under national SAF schemes. Scope: priority EU, UK, US, and Singapore lanes. Deadline: within 6 months or next contract renewal.

**5. Client Sustainability Reporting Integration**
For high-value cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury, film/TV), client Scope 3 emissions reporting is a growing expectation. The workspace should: (a) develop a standard SAF attribution disclosure document for clients using IATA SAF Registry certificate data; (b) ensure the workspace's own Scope 3 (Category 4 — upstream transportation) emissions methodology accommodates SAF emission reduction claims; (c) train account managers and client-facing staff on the difference between CORSIA SAF (minimum 10% lifecycle GHG reduction), EU ReFuelEU SAF (RED III aligned), and UK SAF Mandate criteria (minimum 40% GHG reduction). Training materials are available on-demand via ICAO ACT-SAF training series. Scope: all commercial verticals. Deadline: training within 90 days; reporting integration within 6 months.

*Sources: ICAO SAF page, ICAO, April 2026. https://www.icao.int/SAF; IATA SAF Registry/CADO, April 2026. https://airlines.iata.org/2026/04/14/ensuring-transparency-saf-environmental-benefits; IATA CORSIA Fact Sheet, IATA, December 2025. https://www.iata.org/en/iata-repository/pressroom/fact-sheets/fact-sheet-corsia/*
